title: BPPV follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting positional symptom context, clinician-performed diagnostic assessment, mobility, central nervous system context, home support, fall risk, and factors affecting management without asserting BPPV.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact BPPV assessment section supports positional symptom context, interval change, functional impact, associated or negative features, clinician-performed assessment, and fall-risk modifiers without asserting persistent or recurrent BPPV.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting positional symptom context, clinician-performed diagnostic assessment, mobility, central nervous system context, home support, fall risk, and factors affecting management without asserting BPPV.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (Update)

exact_section.heading: Key action statements 1a–3 — positional assessment, differential, and modifying factors

medication.text: Supports clinician-led outcome reassessment, persistent symptom documentation, safety discussion, questions, and arranged follow-up without generating treatment or a manoeuvre.
medication.rationale: The exact reassessment section supports concerns, clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without selecting a repositioning manoeuvre or medication.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led outcome reassessment, persistent symptom documentation, safety discussion, questions, and arranged follow-up without generating treatment or a manoeuvre.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (Update)

exact_section.heading: Key action statements 7–8b — reassessment, persistent symptoms, and education

sources_used.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (Update)
sources_used.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE vestibular and referral-pathway adaptation

source_limitations: Positional trigger, episode duration, nystagmus, side, manoeuvre, response, falls, neurological symptoms, gait, examination, diagnosis, treatment, rehabilitation, referral, and timing remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
